Convert the current markdown note into an Operon file task?

This will convert the selected file task into a single inline task. Only canonical Operon data is preserved: description, checkbox state, tags, and canonical fields. File body content, comments, custom or non-canonical frontmatter, and other markdown content are not moved into the inline task. After the inline task is created, the source file is moved to Obsidian trash.

Daily Notes, Active File, and Ask Every Time heading keyword. First heading containing this phrase receives new inline tasks. If none exists, Operon creates a ## heading. Specific File mode instead uses the active Core Daily Notes date format, with YYYY-MM-DD as fallback.

Tasks that depend on this task before they can continue.
Contexts or environments relevant to the task.
Cancellation date set when the task is marked cancelled.
Completion date set when the task is marked done.
Due date used to show deadlines and due markers.

End datetime that limits recurrence generation.
Scheduled start datetime used by calendar and timing views.
Tracked work duration stored in seconds.
Estimated work duration stored in seconds.
External web links associated with the task.
Location coordinates stored as latitude, longitude text and shown as a map chip.
Short note or annotation shown alongside the task.
Internal unique task ID used to keep the file task linked to Operon systems.
Parent task ID used to build task hierarchies.
Priority value used for sorting, views, and planning decisions.
Completion percentage used in views and summaries.
Absolute local reminder date and time values associated with the task.
Dynamic reminder rules relative to the task's start time, end time, due date, start date, or scheduled date.
Recurring rule that controls future task generation.
Internal recurring series ID used to group generated occurrences.
Workflow state used by pipelines, filters, and task actions.
Color value used in Operon task views.
Icon name used in Operon task views.
Timezone used to interpret scheduled datetime values.
Automatic tracked duration total including child tasks.
Automatic estimate total including child tasks.
Completed tracking sessions stored as datetime ranges.

Search Lucide icon or leave blank

Each Operon task field has an internal canonical key and a visible property name. The visible name is used in file-task YAML, inline task metadata, and task UI surfaces. Renaming the visible property changes how the field appears without changing the canonical key used by views, automation, and migrations.
Hide this field from the rendered metadata section shown in file tasks and inline metadata. The value is still saved and usable by Operon.
Property name used in file-task YAML and inline task metadata (editable)

Data type used for this field in file-task YAML, inline task metadata, and Operon views, such as text, number, date, checkbox, or list. This helps Operon choose the right editor, validation, and display format.
Values used in file-task YAML are shared with matching inline task fields, so they can appear as suggestions while editing inline tasks, or the other way around.

Workflow statuses, colors, icons, and automatic status behavior.

Pipelines define the workflow stages your tasks move through. Each status can have its own color, icon, and automatic behavior. Choose the statuses Operon should switch tasks to when a date is assigned, time tracking starts, a task is finished, or a task is cancelled. Status values use the format Pipeline.Status, for example Project.InProgress.
Define priority rank, order, colors, icons, and the default priority for new tasks. Priority order is used for sorting, views, and planning decisions.

{{count}} existing serial already uses this prefix. Stored task-number assignments will stay unchanged; only the visible prefix will change.

Define read-only visual serials for a parent task tree. The real operonId is unchanged. Prefixes use 1-5 ASCII letters and keep your casing; the dash is automatic. Letters plus numbers always total 7 characters, excluding the dash. Nested serials win over parent serials, and generated numbers are automatic, persistent, and not manually editable.
